rationale: Add a small procedural guard to prefer short command forms and temp files, reducing Windows CLI arg-length risk without touching code.

edits.content:
{
  "schema": "brainflow.procedure.v1",
  "name": "cli_arg_length_guard",
  "goal": "Reduce Windows CLI argument-length failures by preferring temp files and short commands.",
  "rules": [
    "When constructing CLI commands, avoid long inline JSON or prompts; write payloads to a temp file and pass the file path.",
    "Prefer @file or --input-file style flags when available.",
    "If a command line exceeds ~6k characters, refactor to use file inputs or environment variables.",
    "Keep paths short (use workspace-relative paths when possible)."
  ]
}

risks: If a downstream component expects inline JSON only, it may ignore this guidance until code is updated to read the procedure.
expected_benefit: Lower incidence of Windows CLI arg-length errors and more reliable execution when large prompts or payloads are used.
